title: psorospermum suffruticosum
intro: Psorospermum suffruticosum is a plant species commonly known as the Cinchona bark or Quinine tree. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat malaria and other fevers, due to the presence of quinine in its bark. The health benefits of Psorospermum suffruticosum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can also aid in reducing fever and relieving pain. In herbal preparations, the bark of the plant is typically dried and processed into a powder or tea, which can be used to treat various ailments.
